req.title: Submit leave request — variant 3
req.statement: A full-time employee shall be able to submit a leave request for between 1 and 30 business days; requests spanning more than 30 days require hr-admin approval. Additionally, the action must emit a webhook event for external consumers.
error_pathways: 422 when request exceeds 30 days without hr-admin

doc.reasoning: The requirement defines four distinct test-worthy behaviours: (1) happy-path submission within 1-30 business days, (2) boundary at exactly 30 business days, (3) rejection when start_date > end_date (400), (4) rejection when leave overlaps an existing request (409), and (5) rejection when duration exceeds 30 days without hr-admin approval (422). A webhook emission must be verified in the positive case. EP covers the normal range; BVA covers the 30-day upper boundary; NEG covers the three documented error pathways; DT captures the hr-admin approval guard; STC verifies the state machine transition from PENDING→APPROVED when manager acts. Together these satisfy the minimum suite requirement (positive, negative, boundary) with full traceability.

tests.name: test_submit_leave_request_valid_within_30_days
preconditions: User is authenticated as a full-time employee
No existing leave overlaps the chosen date range
Webhook listener is active and reachable
actions: Fill start_date with a date that is 5 business days from today
Fill end_date with a date that is 9 business days from today (5-day span)
expected: HTTP 201 or UI success banner is shown
Leave request record is created with status PENDING
Manager receives a notification
Notification email is dispatched
Webhook event leave_request.created is emitted with correct payload
tests.executable:
def test_submit_leave_request_valid_within_30_days(
    auth_client,
    full_time_employee,
    webhook_listener,
    clean_leave_state,
):
    start, end = <calc_business_days>(offset_start=5, span=5)
    payload = {"start_date": start, "end_date": end, "type": "annual"}

    response = auth_client.post("/leave-requests/new", json=payload)

    assert response.status_code == 201
    body = response.json()
    assert body["status"] == "PENDING"
    assert body["employee_id"] == full_time_employee["id"]

    <assert_manager_notified>(full_time_employee["manager_id"])
    <assert_email_sent>(recipient=full_time_employee["email"])

    event = webhook_listener.wait_for_event("leave_request.created", timeout=5)
    assert event is not None
    assert event["data"]["leave_request_id"] == body["id"]

tests.name: test_submit_leave_request_exactly_30_business_days
preconditions: User is authenticated as a full-time employee
No existing leave overlaps the chosen date range
Webhook listener is active and reachable
actions: Set start_date and end_date to span exactly 30 business days
Submit the form without hr-admin token
expected: HTTP 201 or UI success banner is shown
Leave request created with status PENDING (no hr-admin approval required at boundary)
Webhook event leave_request.created emitted
trace: R-WEB-059 / upper boundary of self-service window (30 business days)
R-WEB-059 / requests <=30 days do NOT require hr-admin approval
tests.executable:
def test_submit_leave_request_exactly_30_business_days(
    auth_client,
    full_time_employee,
    webhook_listener,
    clean_leave_state,
):
    start, end = <calc_business_days>(offset_start=1, span=30)
    payload = {"start_date": start, "end_date": end, "type": "annual"}

    response = auth_client.post("/leave-requests/new", json=payload)

    assert response.status_code == 201
    body = response.json()
    assert body["status"] == "PENDING"
    assert body.get("requires_hr_admin") is not True

    event = webhook_listener.wait_for_event("leave_request.created", timeout=5)
    assert event is not None
    assert event["data"]["duration_days"] == 30

tests.name: test_submit_leave_request_start_after_end_returns_400
preconditions: User is authenticated as a full-time employee
actions: Set start_date to a date AFTER end_date
expected: HTTP 400 Bad Request is returned
Response body contains error code DATE_RANGE_INVALID or equivalent
No leave request record is created
No webhook event is emitted
trace: R-WEB-059 / error pathway: 400 when start_date > end_date
tests.executable:
def test_submit_leave_request_start_after_end_returns_400(
    auth_client,
    full_time_employee,
    webhook_listener,
):
    start, end = <calc_business_days>(offset_start=10, span=-3)  # end before start
    payload = {"start_date": start, "end_date": end, "type": "annual"}

    response = auth_client.post("/leave-requests/new", json=payload)

    assert response.status_code == 400
    body = response.json()
    assert "error" in body
    assert body["error"] in ("DATE_RANGE_INVALID", "invalid_date_range")

    no_event = webhook_listener.wait_for_event("leave_request.created", timeout=2)
    assert no_event is None

tests.name: test_submit_leave_request_overlapping_existing_returns_409
preconditions: User is authenticated as a full-time employee
An approved leave request already exists for the employee covering the target date range
actions: Seed an existing leave record overlapping the intended dates via fixture
Submit a new request with the same overlapping date range
expected: HTTP 409 Conflict is returned
Response body references OVERLAPPING_LEAVE or equivalent
No duplicate leave request record is created
trace: R-WEB-059 / error pathway: 409 when overlapping existing leave
tests.executable:
def test_submit_leave_request_overlapping_existing_returns_409(
    auth_client,
    full_time_employee,
    existing_leave_fixture,
):
    start, end = existing_leave_fixture["start"], existing_leave_fixture["end"]
    payload = {"start_date": start, "end_date": end, "type": "annual"}

    response = auth_client.post("/leave-requests/new", json=payload)

    assert response.status_code == 409
    body = response.json()
    assert body.get("error") in ("OVERLAPPING_LEAVE", "leave_overlap")

    all_requests = auth_client.get("/leave-requests").json()
    overlapping = [
        r for r in all_requests
        if r["start_date"] == start and r["end_date"] == end
    ]
    assert len(overlapping) == 1  # only the pre-seeded one

tests.name: test_submit_leave_request_exceeds_30_days_without_hr_admin_returns_422
preconditions: User is authenticated as a full-time employee
No existing leave overlaps the chosen date range
Request is submitted WITHOUT hr-admin approval token
actions: Set start_date and end_date to span 31 business days
Submit the form without attaching an hr-admin approval reference
expected: HTTP 422 Unprocessable Entity is returned
Response body contains HR_ADMIN_APPROVAL_REQUIRED or equivalent
No leave request record is created
No webhook event is emitted
trace: R-WEB-059 / error pathway: 422 when request exceeds 30 days without hr-admin
R-WEB-059 / decision: duration > 30 AND no hr-admin => reject
tests.executable:
def test_submit_leave_request_exceeds_30_days_without_hr_admin_returns_422(
    auth_client,
    full_time_employee,
    webhook_listener,
    clean_leave_state,
):
    start, end = <calc_business_days>(offset_start=1, span=31)
    payload = {"start_date": start, "end_date": end, "type": "annual"}
    # Explicitly ensure no hr_admin_approval_id is present
    payload.pop("hr_admin_approval_id", None)

    response = auth_client.post("/leave-requests/new", json=payload)

    assert response.status_code == 422
    body = response.json()
    assert body.get("error") in (
        "HR_ADMIN_APPROVAL_REQUIRED",
        "hr_admin_required",
    )

    no_event = webhook_listener.wait_for_event("leave_request.created", timeout=2)
    assert no_event is None

tests.name: test_leave_request_state_transitions_pending_to_approved
preconditions: User is authenticated as a full-time employee
Manager account is available and authenticated
Leave request for 5 business days has been submitted and is in PENDING state
actions: Submit a valid leave request (5 business days) to obtain a PENDING record
Manager approves the leave request via the approval endpoint
expected: Leave request transitions from PENDING to APPROVED
Employee receives an approval notification email
Webhook event leave_request.approved is emitted
trace: R-WEB-059 / webhook event emitted on approval
tests.executable:
def test_leave_request_state_transitions_pending_to_approved(
    auth_client,
    manager_client,
    full_time_employee,
    webhook_listener,
    clean_leave_state,
):
    start, end = <calc_business_days>(offset_start=2, span=5)
    create_resp = auth_client.post(
        "/leave-requests/new",
        json={"start_date": start, "end_date": end, "type": "annual"},
    )
    assert create_resp.status_code == 201
    leave_id = create_resp.json()["id"]
    assert create_resp.json()["status"] == "PENDING"

    approve_resp = manager_client.patch(
        f"/leave-requests/{leave_id}/approve"
    )
    assert approve_resp.status_code == 200
    assert approve_resp.json()["status"] == "APPROVED"

    <assert_email_sent>(recipient=full_time_employee["email"], subject_contains="approved")
    event = webhook_listener.wait_for_event("leave_request.approved", timeout=5)
    assert event is not None
    assert event["data"]["leave_request_id"] == leave_id
